task_completion.description: The agent accesses the correct Jitsi Meet web configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) or terminal command is visible, showing the file path `/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js` being opened or modified.

task_completion.description: The agent configures the server to start with audio muted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal output shows the setting `startWithAudioMuted: true` being added, uncommented, or modified in the config.js file.

task_completion.description: The agent configures the server to start with video muted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal output shows the setting `startWithVideoMuted: true` being added, uncommented, or modified in the config.js file.

task_completion.description: The agent opens Firefox and navigates to the specified meeting room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Firefox browser window is open, and the URL bar or Jitsi Meet interface clearly shows the room name 'QuietMeeting' (e.g., `.../QuietMeeting`).

task_completion.description: The agent verifies that the 'quiet start' policy is active in the meeting room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the Jitsi Meet pre-join screen or inside the 'QuietMeeting' room in Firefox, both the microphone and camera icons are visibly in a muted/disabled state (typically indicated by a diagonal slash through the icons or a red background).

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually used the Firefox browser to verify the configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: A genuine Firefox graphical window is visible rendering the Jitsi Meet web interface, rather than just terminal outputs like curl commands.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent modified the actual configuration file and not a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path visible in the text editor or terminal command exactly matches `/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js`.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the muted state visually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Firefox Developer Tools (Inspector/Console) are NOT open and being used to manually alter the HTML/CSS classes of the microphone and camera buttons.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct configuration file path is exactly `/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js`. The agent must set two specific boolean configuration options: `startWithAudioMuted: true` and `startWithVideoMuted: true`. The target meeting room to verify the configuration is named 'QuietMeeting'. When properly configured, the Jitsi Meet UI will show the microphone and camera icons as muted (usually with a strike-through or red color) by default when joining the room.
